title: Allow searching $TZDIR for timezone data
body:
glibc supports the environment variable as additional search path, means we allow to override the `/usr/share/zoneinfo` path by setting the variable.

Analogous change to https://github.com/containers/common/pull/1772


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?


```release-note
Added `TZDIR` environment variable support to modify the global timezone search path of `/usr/share/zoneinfo`.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [saschagrunert] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Also,  it doesn't seem to be working:
```console
$ mkdir -p /tmp/zoneinfo/Abc
$ cp /usr/share/zoneinfo/Pacific/Chatham /tmp/zoneinfo/Abc/Def

$ TZDIR=/tmp/zoneinfo TZ=Abc/Def date
Thu Dec 21 03:15:13 AM +1345 2023       <--- OK, it works on f39

$ TZDIR=/tmp/zoneinfo bin/podman run --rm --tz=Abc/Def quay.io/libpod/testimage:20221018 date
Error: running container create option: finding timezone: unknown time zone Abc/Def
^^^^^ but not with podman
```

@sohankunkerkar @rhatdan @vrothberg @edsantiago @Luap99 considering https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pull/7589, do we want to move the timezone related bits into c/common helper functions and reuse them in CRI-O and Podman?

> @sohankunkerkar @rhatdan @vrothberg @edsantiago @Luap99 considering [cri-o/cri-o#7589](https://github.com/cri-o/cri-o/pull/7589), do we want to move the timezone related bits into c/common helper functions and reuse them in CRI-O and Podman?

Yeah, that's a good idea. We can reuse the `setContainerTimeZone` and `copyTimezoneFile` functions by placing them in c/common.
Awesome, let me close this one then and vendor c/common code once it is there.
